perturbation.original_text: 1. "Confidential Information" shall mean all information disclosed to Recipient by Discloser or its agents or employees in any manner, whether orally, visually or in tangible form (including, without limitation, documents, devices and computer readable media) and all copies thereof, whether created by Discloser or Recipient. Tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information shall be marked, to the extent practicable, by Discloser as "Confidential," "Proprietary" or the substantial equivalent thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. "Confidential Information" shall mean information disclosed to Recipient by Discloser or its agents or employees. Tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information shall be marked by Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "in any manner, whether orally, visually or in tangible form (including, without limitation, documents, devices and computer readable media) and all copies thereof, whether created by Discloser or Recipient" from the definition of 'Confidential Information' it introduces ambiguity. The phrase "shall be marked by Discloser" contradicts the "to the extent practicable" clause which created an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. "Confidential Information" shall mean information disclosed to Recipient by Discloser or its agents or employees. Tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information shall be marked by Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits key elements from the definition of 'Confidential Information,' specifically removing 'in any manner, whether orally, visually or in tangible form (including, without limitation, documents, devices and computer readable media) and all copies thereof, whether created by Discloser or Recipient'. Additionally, it removes the qualifier 'to the extent practicable' regarding marking tangible materials. This omission creates a contradiction because the changed text now requires all tangible materials to be marked, which directly clashes with the original flexibility of marking them only 'to the extent practicable.' The Contradicted Text and Changed Text are the same, meaning the change has a direct contradiction in the modified clause.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Recipients shall use Confidential Information solely for the purpose (the "Permitted Purpose") specified at the end of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Recipients shall use Confidential Information for a business purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "solely for the purpose (the "Permitted Purpose") specified at the end of this Agreement." and replacing it with "for a business purpose." The definition contradicts the 'Permitted Purpose' that is later still defined in the contract. This makes it unclear if the recipient can use the information for purposes beyond evaluating a joint venture, etc, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Recipients shall use Confidential Information for a business purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts the use of Confidential Information to a 'Permitted Purpose' defined elsewhere. The changed text broadens this to 'a business purpose,' directly contradicting the previous restriction. The contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, further supporting the change's contradictory nature, as it removes the original restriction to the Permitted Purpose. According to the explanation, this makes it unclear if the recipient can use the information for purposes beyond the Permitted Purpose, creating an in-text contradiction and ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Recipient agrees not to reproduce or copy by any means Confidential Information, except as reasonably required to accomplish such Recipient's Permitted Purpose. Upon written demand by Discloser at any time, Recipient shall return promptly to Discloser or destroy, at Recipient's option, all tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information except that Recipient's counsel may, upon written notice to Disclosure's counsel, retain a single copy of Confidential Information in its records solely for use in dispute resolution or as otherwise required by law. Recipient shall not remove any proprietary rights legend from, and shall, upon Discloser's reasonable written request, add an appropriate proprietary legend to, materials disclosing or embodying Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Recipient agrees not to reproduce or copy Confidential Information. Upon written demand by Discloser at any time, Recipient shall return promptly to Discloser or destroy, at Recipient's option, all tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information. Recipient shall not remove any proprietary rights legend from, and shall, upon Discloser's reasonable written request, add an appropriate proprietary legend to, materials disclosing or embodying Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase "except as reasonably required to accomplish such Recipient's Permitted Purpose except that Recipient's counsel may, upon written notice to Disclosure's counsel, retain a single copy of Confidential Information in its records solely for use in dispute resolution or as otherwise required by law", now imposes an absolute ban on reproduction. If the Permitted Purpose defined later in the document requires some level of reproduction, this section now contradicts that purpose, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Recipient agrees not to reproduce or copy Confidential Information. Upon written demand by Discloser at any time, Recipient shall return promptly to Discloser or destroy, at Recipient's option, all tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information. Recipient shall not remove any proprietary rights legend from, and shall, upon Discloser's reasonable written request, add an appropriate proprietary legend to, materials disclosing or embodying Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed reproduction for the Recipient's Permitted Purpose and retained counsel in specific cases. The changed text imposes an absolute ban on reproduction. If the Permitted Purpose, as defined elsewhere, requires reproduction, this creates a direct contradiction with the changed clause. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, because it now stands in contrast to the Permitted Purpose, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Discloser understands that Recipient is an ice cream manufacturer and engaged in distribution (and with arrangements with distributors or subdistributors) and that planned activities in all such areas that are independently developed, pursued or acquired by the Recipient may contain ideas and concepts similar or identical to those contained in Discloser's Confidential Information. Discloser agrees that entering into this Agreement shall not preclude Recipient from developing, pursuing or acquiring projects similar to Discloser's, without obligation to Discloser, provided Recipient does not breach its obligations to Discloser under this Agreement or use the Confidential Information to develop or pursue such projects. The parties agree that unless and until a definitive written agreement between the Company and Ben & Jerry's has been executed and delivered relating to a specific transaction (which agreement contains customary conditions, representations, warranties and covenants for an agreement of that kind), neither party shall be under any obligation whatsoever with respect to such a transaction by virtue of this or any written or oral communication with respect to such a transaction by it or any of its respective Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Discloser understands that Recipient is an ice cream manufacturer and engaged in distribution (and with arrangements with distributors or subdistributors) and that planned activities in all such areas that are independently developed, pursued or acquired by the Recipient may contain ideas and concepts similar or identical to those contained in Discloser's Confidential Information. Discloser agrees that entering into this Agreement shall not preclude Recipient from developing, pursuing or acquiring projects similar to Discloser's, without obligation to Discloser, provided Recipient does not breach its obligations to Discloser under this Agreement. The parties agree that unless and until a definitive written agreement between the Company and Ben & Jerry's has been executed and delivered relating to a specific transaction (which agreement contains customary conditions, representations, warranties and covenants for an agreement of that kind), neither party shall be under any obligation whatsoever with respect to such a transaction by virtue of this or any written or oral communication with respect to such a transaction by it or any of its respective Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "or use the Confidential Information to develop or pursue such projects", this creates an in-text contradiction. The agreement generally aims to protect confidential information and the recipient is now free to use said information to pursue similar projects. Thus the general definition of confidential information is not applicable, creating ambiguity and an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Discloser understands that Recipient is an ice cream manufacturer and engaged in distribution (and with arrangements with distributors or subdistributors) and that planned activities in all such areas that are independently developed, pursued or acquired by the Recipient may contain ideas and concepts similar or identical to those contained in Discloser's Confidential Information. Discloser agrees that entering into this Agreement shall not preclude Recipient from developing, pursuing or acquiring projects similar to Discloser's, without obligation to Discloser, provided Recipient does not breach its obligations to Discloser under this Agreement. The parties agree that unless and until a definitive written agreement between the Company and Ben & Jerry's has been executed and delivered relating to a specific transaction (which agreement contains customary conditions, representations, warranties and covenants for an agreement of that kind), neither party shall be under any obligation whatsoever with respect to such a transaction by virtue of this or any written or oral communication with respect to such a transaction by it or any of its respective Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 8 prevented the Recipient from using the Discloser's Confidential Information to develop or pursue similar projects. By removing the phrase "or use the Confidential Information to develop or pursue such projects" in the changed text of section 8, the Recipient is now implicitly allowed to use the confidential information, creating a direct contradiction with the general intent to protect said information, thus triggering a strong in-text contradiction as the recipient is now free to do something the original text explicitly prevented, based on the perturbation explanation provided.
